bagna cauda
Piedmontese hot dip made from anchovies, garlic, and olive oil
pad thai
Sauce base is built on fish sauce (nam pla)
puttanesca
Italian tomato sauce traditionally built with anchovies melted into the olive oil
tom yum
Thai hot-sour soup base is seasoned with fish sauce
caesar
Classic Caesar dressing is traditionally emulsified with anchovy paste or fillets
okonomiyaki
Batter is typically made with bonito (dashi) stock and topped with bonito flakes; sauce is Worcestershire-style
som tam
Green papaya salad dressing is fish-sauce based
nicoise
Salade Nicoise is traditionally garnished with whole anchovy fillets
takoyaki
Batter contains dashi and it's topped with dried bonito (katsuobushi) flakes
pad see ew
Thai stir-fried noodle sauce typically seasoned with fish sauce
xo sauce
Chinese luxury condiment made from dried scallops, dried fish, and dried shrimp
bun cha
Vietnamese grilled-pork dish served with nuoc mam (fish sauce) dipping broth
chawanmushi
Japanese savory egg custard steamed in bonito-kelp dashi
larb
Lao/Thai minced-meat salad dressed with fish sauce
bloody mary
Classic recipes call for Worcestershire sauce, which contains anchovy
cha gio
Vietnamese fried spring rolls conventionally served with a fish-sauce dipping sauce
goi cuon
Vietnamese fresh spring rolls conventionally served with fish-sauce-based nuoc cham
kimchi
Traditionally fermented with fish sauce or salted anchovy/shrimp (jeotgal)
miso soup
Standard miso soup stock is made with bonito-flake dashi
pinakbet
Filipino vegetable dish typically seasoned with bagoong (fermented fish/shrimp paste)
yakisoba
Sauce is Worcestershire-style and dish is often topped with bonito flakes
rarebit
Welsh rarebit cheese sauce commonly includes a dash of Worcestershire sauce
